Scenario.title: SPACEHOP Activity: Multi-Stage Cyber Espionage in Communications Sector
Scenario.overview: Communications Complex A sophisticated multi-stage attack targeting communications infrastructure leverages AI-driven reconnaissance, credential theft, and lateral movement to disrupt operations and exfiltrate sensitive data.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Target Artifact (Tasks), Application Log, Network Traffic, Windows Registry, OS Process, Cloud Instance, Credential Store, File
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Artificial Intelligence, Application Log Content, Network Traffic Content, Windows Registry Key Creation, Process Injection, Process Creation, Reg.exe Utility, Pass the Hash, File Modification, Network Connection Creation, Network Traffic Content

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker deploys AI algorithms to analyze communication system task artifacts, identifying vulnerabilities in network protocols and configuration weaknesses.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: Malicious logic is embedded into application logs to establish Operational Relay Boxes (ORBs) for covert command-and-control operations.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2023-36033 (Suggested): Microsoft Windows Desktop Window Manager (DWM) Core Library contains an unspecified vulnerability that allows for privilege escalation.
stage.description: Phishing campaigns deliver malicious links to employee accounts, compromising credentials through socially engineered email interactions.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2022-44877 (Suggested): CWP Control Web Panel (formerly CentOS Web Panel) contains an OS command injection vulnerability that allows remote attackers to execute commands via shell metacharacters in the login parameter.
stage.capec: CAPEC-70: Try Common or Default Usernames and Passwords
stage.description: Payloads are executed using default credentials harvested from network traffic, exploiting weak authentication mechanisms.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: Scheduled tasks are created in the Windows Registry to maintain persistent access post-compromise.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Privileges are escalated by manipulating application log entries to bypass security restrictions and access restricted resources.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Malicious code is injected into legitimate OS processes to hide malicious activities from detection mechanisms.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: Login credentials are captured during communication sessions by altering cloud deployment tools to intercept sensitive data.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Network traffic is monitored using Reg.exe utilities to map internal communication networks and identify high-value targets.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-461: Web Services API Signature Forgery Leveraging Hash Function Extension Weakness
stage.description: Stolen credentials are used with pass-the-hash techniques to move laterally across the network and compromise additional systems.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: Sensitive communication records are gathered by modifying files to extract confidential documents and data.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Data is exfiltrated through leased VPS networks using UDP fragmentation to evade traditional network monitoring tools.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Communication services are disrupted by corrupting network traffic to disable critical hardware and cause operational outages.

Scenario.summary: The SPACEHOP campaign employs a complex, multi-phase attack leveraging AI reconnaissance, credential theft, and network exploitation to infiltrate communications infrastructure, ultimately causing operational disruption and data exfiltration.
